content.text:
All tests pass. The current implementation `return []` already satisfies the failing test "should kill a single live cell with 0 neighbors" — when given `[[0, 0]]` (a single live cell with 0 neighbors), returning `[]` correctly represents the cell dying from underpopulation.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: No changes needed. The existing stub `return [];` in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_12-13-59_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts` already satisfies the test.
- Result: All tests pass (2 passing, 6 todo)
- Approach: This is the most minimal possible implementation — a hardcoded empty array return. It satisfies both the empty-grid test and the single-cell-dies test because the expected output for both is `[]`. No new logic was added; this maintains strict TDD discipline by avoiding any implementation beyond what the tests demand.

Relevant files:
- `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_12-13-59_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts`
